title: Excel Import/Export — NXGN Actions
purpose: **Scope**: This document describes the patterns for Excel download (export) and upload (import) of entity data. Based on Capstone's GemBox.Spreadsheet patterns, adapted for Actions' vertical slice structure.

examples.name: Column Definitions Example 1
queries the database — all data is passed in.
When export requires additional lookup data beyond the entities
the database — the calling endpoint passes the result to the SaveCommand.
- Errors are per-row/per-column with descriptive messages
Download Endpoint Example 1
Upload Endpoint Example 1
Upload Endpoint Example 2
These live in Nxgn.Actions.Api/Excel/ (or a shared infrastructure location)
DI Registration Example 1
Decision Tree: Does This Feature Need Excel? Example 1
examples.code:
Does the feature need bulk import from spreadsheets?
├── YES → Add UploadExcel endpoint + Importer + ImporterContext
│         (batch EntityMapper + SaveCommand are already batch-primary)
├── NO  → Skip upload files
│
Does the feature need data export to spreadsheets?
├── YES → Add DownloadExcel endpoint + Exporter (+ ExporterContext if needed)
├── NO  → Skip download files
│
Does the feature need both?
├── YES → Add all 5 Excel files + 2 endpoints
│         (ExcelColumnDefs shared between exporter and importer)

sections.title: Overview
content: Excel operations follow a 5-file pattern per feature:
Plus two endpoints and their supporting files:

sections.title: Column Definitions
content: Column definitions are static, declarative metadata used by both exporters and importers.
public static readonly ExcelColumnDef Name = new("Name");
public static readonly ExcelColumnDef Priority = new("Priority");
public static readonly ExcelColumnDef ThresholdDays = new("Threshold Days");
public static readonly ExcelColumnDef Tier = new("Tier");
public static readonly ExcelColumnDef NotificationTarget = new("Notification Target");
public static readonly ExcelColumnGroupDef PropertiesGroup = new(
- `ExcelColumnDef(string displayName)` — auto-derives PascalCase key from display name
- `ExcelColumnGroupDef(string groupName, params ExcelColumnDef[] columns)` — groups columns under a header
- `All` property iterates all columns in display order (used by both exporter and importer)

sections.title: Excel Exporter
content: The exporter is a DI-registered class that transforms entities to Excel bytes. It never
queries the database — all data is passed in.
- `GenerateExcel` receives entities and language IDs — never queries DB
- For complex exports needing additional data, use an `ExporterContext` POCO + `ExporterContextService`
When export requires additional lookup data beyond the entities:
public required IList<WorkflowDefinition> Workflows { get; init; }
await using var db = await dbFactory.CreateDbContextAsync(ct);

sections.title: Excel Importer
content: The importer parses Excel rows into DTOs with per-row validation. It never saves to
the database — the calling endpoint passes the result to the SaveCommand.
// Build column index from header row
var dto = new EscalationRuleDTO { Id = Guid.Empty };
- All DTOs get `Id = Guid.Empty` (create mode — the SaveCommand handles upsert via EntityMapper)
- Context service pre-loads lookup maps for validation (e.g., valid workflow names, priority values)
- Never saves to DB — the endpoint handles that
- Errors are per-row/per-column with descriptive messages
public required Dictionary<string, Guid> WorkflowsByName { get; init; }
public required HashSet<string> ValidPriorities { get; init; }
await using var db = await dbFactory.CreateDbContextAsync(ct);

sections.title: Download Endpoint
content: - Three-step pattern: load entities → generate Excel → send bytes
- For complex exports, add context loading step between load and generate

sections.title: Upload Endpoint
content: // 3. If parsing succeeded, save via batch command
// Request — Actions uses KeyedDTO base (no TenantRequest)
// Validator — uses Validator<T> (Actions convention, not TenantRequestValidator<T>)
- Importer returns `ExcelUploadResult<DTO>` — endpoint checks `IsValid` before saving
- SaveCommand receives the **batch** `List<DTO>` — this is why EntityMapper is batch-primary

sections.title: Shared Framework Types
content: These live in `Nxgn.Actions.Api/Excel/` (or a shared infrastructure location):
public record ExcelColumnDef(string DisplayName, string Key)
public ExcelColumnGroupDef(string groupName, params ExcelColumnDef[] columns)
public string ErrorMessage { get; set; } = string.Empty;
public string WarningMessage { get; set; } = string.Empty;

sections.title: Decision Tree: Does This Feature Need Excel?
content: Does the feature need bulk import from spreadsheets?
├── YES → Add UploadExcel endpoint + Importer + ImporterContext
Does the feature need data export to spreadsheets?
├── YES → Add DownloadExcel endpoint + Exporter (+ ExporterContext if needed)
├── YES → Add all 5 Excel files + 2 endpoints

sections.title: See Also
content: - [Commands](commands.md) — SaveCommand batch signature used by upload endpoint
- [EntityMapper](entity-mapper.md) — Batch-primary MapAsync used for bulk import
- [Service Registration](service-registration.md) — DI registration for Excel classes
